title: B1 Listening Part 1 - Test 28
instructions: For each question, listen to the short recording and choose the correct picture: A, B or C.

questions.context: You hear two people choosing food at a food stall.
questions.audio_script: Man: Have you chosen what to eat at the food stall? The queue is moving quickly. || Woman: I nearly chose the noodles, but noodles are too hot. || Man: What about the rice? It looks popular today. || Woman: I thought about it, but rice is already cold. [1]I’m going to have the vegetable wrap[/1]. It should be enough before our journey. || Man: Good choice. I’ll order mine after you, and then we can find a table near the window. || Man: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two friends making plans after school.
questions.audio_script: Woman: Are we still going to the skate park after school? || Man: I don't think so. My skateboard broke yesterday. || Woman: We could try the river instead. || Man: Maybe, but it is too muddy. My neighbour has a spare set, so [1]we're going to the mini golf course this afternoon instead[/1]. || Woman: That sounds fine. Shall we meet outside the bus station? || Man: Yes, at four. Bring your student card, because they sometimes ask for it at the desk. || Man: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a present for my teacher.
questions.audio_script: Girl: I still need a present for my teacher. I thought about buying a mug, but that didn't seem special. || Boy: What about a calendar? You mentioned that last week. || Girl: I checked, but the shop only had expensive ones. In the end, [1]I bought box of pencils because the classroom always runs out[/1]. || Boy: That sounds perfect. Useful presents are often better than decorations. || Girl: I hope so. I’ll wrap it tonight after dinner, and take it over tomorrow. || Girl: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about travelling to the swimming pool.
questions.audio_script: Boy: How are you getting to the swimming pool on Saturday? || Girl: I was going to walk, but I have too much to carry. || Boy: What about the bike? || Girl: True, but my tyre needs air. Dad is busy, but [1]I’m going to go by car because Mum can drop me there on her way to work[/1]. || Boy: Good idea. I’ll meet you by the main entrance at ten, so we can go in together. Don’t forget your ticket. || Boy: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a work shift at a ice cream stall.
questions.audio_script: Woman: Are you working at the ice cream stall this weekend? || Boy: Yes, but not on Monday lunch as usual. The manager changed the rota because the freezer is being repaired. || Woman: So when are you going in? || Boy: I can’t do Tuesday evening, and Wednesday morning is already covered, so [1]I’m doing the Tuesday lunchtime shift[/1]. || Woman: That’s not too bad. You’ll still have some free time later. || Boy: Exactly. I can finish my homework then. || Boy: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two friends talking about a visit to a adventure park.
questions.audio_script: Woman: What did you do at the adventure park? I thought you were trying the zip wire. || Man: We planned to, but it was too windy. || Woman: Did you try kayaking? || Man: No, because the lake was closed. In the end, [1]we spent the afternoon doing mini golf[/1]. It was more fun than I expected. || Woman: Maybe I’ll go next time. || Man: You should. They lend you everything you need, and the staff are friendly. || Man: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a lost keys.
questions.audio_script: Man: Have you found your keys yet? I know you were worried about it this morning. || Woman: Not really. I checked the garage, because I was sure I had it there after helping my uncle. || Man: And what about the café? You went there afterwards, didn't you? || Woman: Yes, but nobody had seen it. Then my neighbour called and said [1]my keys was in the front garden by the gate[/1]. I’m going to pick it up before it closes. || Man: Great. At least you know where it is now, and you can stop looking everywhere.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you found your keys yet? I know you were worried about it this morning.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not really. I checked the garage, because I was sure I had it there after helping my uncle.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: And what about the café? You went there afterwards, didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but nobody had seen it. Then my neighbour called and said my keys was in the front garden by the gate. I’m going to pick it up before it closes.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Great. At least you know where it is now, and you can stop looking everywhere.
